EVENT_NARRATIVE: Additional wind damage east of the path of the tornado was noted continuing eastward towards Laurel. This was the result of straight-line winds associated with the rear-flank downdraft which also caused the loss of all of the metal roofing on one commercial building as well as the partial loss of metal roofing on another commercial building in Laurel as well as additional tree damage south of Chipman Pond.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist low-level air mass north of an approaching warm front resulted in widespread dense fog across portions of northern Delaware starting during the evening of May 15th and continuing into the early morning of May 16th. The fog dissipated after sunrise.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dense fog with visibility 1/4 mile or less was observed for several hours at Wilmington Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A stationary front over the region resulted in two rounds of severe thunderstorms. The first was in the late morning through mid day. After a few hours lull, a second round of thunderstorms occurred late in the afternoon through the evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: On Friday May 16th, an EF-1 tornado occurred beginning in Dorchester County, Maryland before crossing into Sussex County, Delaware. While the tornado was in Sussex County, tree damage occurred in Phillips Landing Park. A tree was snapped and twisted near the base of its trunk with additional tree damage in the Cherry Walk Woods neighborhood and along Phillips Landing Road. Additional trees were damaged along Shell Bridge Road with a tree snapped near the intersection with Phillips Landing Road. Several large limbs were also downed near Broad Creek, south-southwest of Bethel. The tornado dissipated in this area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms developed along a sea-breeze front. A few storms produced strong microbursts, bringing damaging wind gusts across portions of Delaware.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Measured wind gust of 58 mph with tree limbs down nearby in Bethel from a microburst.  Photo of tree limb down and video showing onset of microburst and severe winds  provided in report.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Storm surge from Hurricane Erin and an upcoming New Moon resulted in moderate to locally major high tide levels on the night of August 21, 2025 across coastal areas of Delaware.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread roadway flooding was reported in the tidal areas of the county, and there were numerous road closures. Many homes and other buildings were surrounded by flood waters with some minor property damage occurring. The tide gauge at Lewes reached 7.70 feet MLLW, the tide gauge at Indian River Inlet reached 6.22 feet MLLW, the tide gauge at Dewey Beach reached 5.18 feet MLLW, and the tide gauge at Rosedale Beach reached 6.27 feet MLLW.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved into a warm and unstable airmass, producing strong to severe thunderstorms and damaging wind gusts. A storm along the sea-breeze also produced a brief tornado.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Damage assessment determined that an EF0 tornado with a maximum wind speed of 65 mph briefly touched down along Omar Rd in Frankford, DE and moved ESE over a heavily forested area towards Shockley Town Rd. Several brief video clips shared by broadcast media and social media showed various angles of a tornado in progress in the heavily forested area between Omar Rd and Shockley Town Rd. Several large branches were twisted and snapped along Omar Rd. Additional damage is possible in the heavily forested area, but the survey team was unable to access the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful coastal storm sat off the Delaware coast, with strong winds pushing water towards the shoreline and into the back bays of Delaware. Moderate to locally major high tide levels were observed over the southern half of the state. Tidal flooding continued over multiple high tide cycles.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread roadway flooding was reported in the tidal areas of the county, and there were numerous road closures. Many homes and other buildings were surrounded by flood waters with some minor property damage occurring. The tide gauge at Lewes reached 7.73 feet MLLW, the tide gauge at Indian River Inlet reached 6.14 feet MLLW, the tide gauge at Dewey Beach reached 4.99 feet MLLW,  the tide gauge at Rosedale Beach reached 6.49 feet MLLW, the tide gauge at South Bethany Beach reached 3.82 feet MLLW, and the tide gauge at Fenwick Island reached 3.65 feet MLLW.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Onshore flow from distant Hurricane Humberto and Imelda resulted in high tides and moderate tidal flooding. Some reports of inundation and road closures came in.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread roadway flooding was reported in the tidal areas of the county, and there were numerous road closures. Many homes and other buildings were surrounded by flood waters with some minor property damage occurring. The tide gauge at Dewey Beach reached 4.38 feet MLLW.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An area of low pressure moved just to the west of Delaware, with onshore easterly flow pushing water towards the New Jersey coast and back bay communities. This resulted in Moderate Tidal Flooding along the southern Delaware coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread roadway flooding was reported in the tidal areas of the county, and there were numerous road closures. Many homes and other buildings were surrounded by flood waters with some minor property damage occurring. The tide gauge at Indian River Inlet reached 5.31 feet MLLW, the tide gauge at Dewey Beach reached 4.42 feet MLLW, the tide gauge at South Bethany Beach reached 3.85 feet MLLW, and the tide gauge at Fenwick Island reached 3.61 feet MLLW.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The first significant winter storm of the season brought widespread light to moderate snowfall accumulations to the region. The heaviest amounts occurred in northern Delmarva. Widespread 1-5 totals were reported across Delaware.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall totals ranged from 1-3. The highest report was 3.4 from Smyrna Landing.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The first significant winter storm of the season brought widespread light to moderate snowfall accumulations to the region. The heaviest amounts occurred in northern Delmarva. Widespread 1-5 totals were reported across Delaware.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall totals ranged from 1-2. The highest report was 2.5 from Bridgeville.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm accompanied by strong warm advection aloft brought a widespread wintry mix of precipitation to portions of the region. A coating of snow and sleet, as well as a coating of freezing rain was reported across much of eastern Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and northern Delmarva. The bulk of the frozen wintry precipitation in Delaware occurred across northern portions of the state, where trace amounts of snow and sleet and a glaze of ice fell.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trace amounts of snow and sleet were reported. A light glaze of freezing rain/freezing drizzle was also reported across the county. As much as 0.15 of ice was reported at the New Castle County Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The first significant winter storm of the season brought widespread light to moderate snowfall accumulations to the region. The heaviest amounts occurred in northern Delmarva. Widespread 1-5 totals were reported across Delaware.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall totals were mostly near 4 across the southern half of the county, amounts near 5 to 6 were reported near and north of I-95. The highest report was 6.8 from Bear. There were several surface transportation accidents. At the Wilmington Airport, 13 flights were canceled and 24 more were delayed.
